page: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge is a high-capacity server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture with Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with 7600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing and cryptography. The server achieved a top-tier rating in the Passmark CPU score with 86301.41 and the Passmark CPU encryption test with 109511.98. Multi-core performance is further validated by a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 123833.48. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 6667.57 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 334.77 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in database operations, achieving 103.85 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and high static web serving throughput at 69.41 GB/sec. Large language model inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds of 189.43 tokens per second for a medium 7B model, though text generation for large 70B models is a relative bottleneck at 2.78 tokens per second. This hardware profile offers high resource density and compute efficiency for multi-threaded processing, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge is a high-capacity compute instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 86301.41, and strong memory read bandwidth of up to 6667.57 GB/sec. It is highly efficient for database operations, showing strong Redis SET performance of 103.85 million operations per second, and excels in static web serving. While prompt processing for medium LLM models is strong, text generation for large 70B models represents a performance tradeoff. This instance is suited for multi-threaded CPU tasks, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services trn1.32xlarge is a high-capacity server featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.

tagline: A high-capacity compute and storage platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive NVMe SSD capacity, and high-speed networking.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz
512 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier Passmark CPU and encryption benchmark performance
Strong Redis database operations and static web serving throughput
Optimized for multi-threaded processing and high-throughput database workloads
